articles.title: Oil steadies and Asian stocks are mostly lower on mixed signs on Iran - AP News
articles.description: Oil prices have steadied and Asian stocks are mostly trading lower, as signs of a de-escalation of the Iran war remain mixed. Tokyo's Nikkei 225 is down 1.2%, and South Korea's Kospi has lost 3.4%. With the Iran war in its fifth week, there is no clear end to…

articles.title: China's factory activity returns to growth, expanding at its sharpest pace in a year - CNBC
articles.description: China's official gauge for manufacturing activity pointed to a better-than-expected expansion in March, snapping two months of declines.

articles.title: Air Canada CEO will retire this year after his English-only crash message was criticized - CNN
articles.description: Air Canada announced Monday that its CEO will retire later this year, after Michael Rousseau was criticized for his English-only message of condolence following this month’s deadly crash in New York.

articles.title: Employees could use 401(k)s to invest in crypto, private equity under Trump plan - The Washington Post
articles.description: A Labor Department proposal would allow more retirement investment in “alternative assets,” a win for Wall Street that critics say is risky for investors.

articles.title: Stock futures slip as oil prices resume their climb; S&P 500 creeps closer to correction territory: Live updates - CNBC
articles.description: The broad market S&P 500 fell for a third consecutive session on Monday as oil prices rose once again.

articles.title: Allbirds to Be Bought by American Exchange for $39 Million - Bloomberg
articles.description: American Exchange Group has agreed to buy all of the assets and intellectual property of once-buzzy footwear brand Allbirds Inc. for $39 million.

articles.title: Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris - SpaceNews
articles.description: For the second time in just over three months, a SpaceX Starlink satellite has generated debris from an apparent on-orbit malfunction.

articles.title: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll - TechCrunch
articles.description: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

articles.title: A Winner in Early Trump Tariffs, Vietnam Thrives in Trade War 2.0 - bloomberg.com
